summary:
Stereotactic radiosurgery is considered highly effective for treating radiation-induced meningiomas, offering excellent tumor control—up to 98% —even in small lesions. Its application remains valid despite concerns over cumulative radiation exposure because outcomes are comparable to other meningioma subtypes and risks are managed through careful patient selection based on standard criteria such as lesion size and location.
